QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_53

question.text: A director of a merchant banker holds shares worth Rs. 8 lakh (nominal value) representing 0.15% of the issuer's paid-up capital. Can the merchant banker lead manage the issue?

answer.text: Yes, because the nominal value is below Rs. 10 lakh
Yes, because the percentage is very small
No, because the holding exceeds the lower of the two thresholds (0.1% of paid-up capital)
No, because any shareholding by a director is prohibited

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The restriction applies when holding exceeds 0.1% of paid-up capital OR shares exceeding Rs. 10 lakh nominal value — whichever threshold is LOWER. Here, 0.15% exceeds the 0.1% threshold, so even though nominal value is below Rs. 10 lakh, the merchant banker cannot lead manage. **The Trap:** Focusing only on the Rs. 10 lakh threshold and ignoring the 0.1% threshold is a common mistake. The regulation uses "whichever is lower" — meaning the more restrictive limit applies. **Cross-Exam Context:** Conflict of interest thresholds are a recurring theme in NISM Series IX and NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check BOTH thresholds before accepting a lead management mandate — the lower one governs.
